posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked: I’ve loved the Durand which has saved many a cork and bottle. Last night was different. Opened a 2001 Raveneau MDT and the neck of the bottle snapped as I turned the Durand to extract the cork. Fortunately no glass fell in the bottle and I salvaged a great quaff.

posts.name: Kris_P@tten
posts.cooked:
Never seen that. Glad you saved the wine.

Just saber it next time.

posts.name: Al Osterheld
posts.cooked:
I’m guessing there was a flaw in the bottle (although, you are a weight lifter).

-Al

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
I’m guessing there was a flaw in the bottle (although, you are a weight lifter).

-Al

hahah. My guess also re the defective neck.

posts.name: Dale Bowers
posts.cooked: Shouldn’t this be combined with the weightlifting thread?

posts.name: Rodrigo Br@g@
posts.cooked: Could be glass flaw. I’ve had this happen a few times with an ah-so and corks that are much bigger than the opening. They force the prongs to exert more pressure onto the side walls making it more prone to break. Usually it happens with younger or synthetic corks as they’re still quite a bit more rigid. Sorry this happened to you Alan. Thankfully you managed to salvage it.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Could be glass flaw. I’ve had this happen a few times with an ah-so and corks that are much bigger than the opening. They force the prongs to exert more pressure onto the side walls making it more prone to break. Usually it happens with younger or synthetic corks as they’re still quite a bit more rigid. Sorry this happened to you Alan. Thankfully you managed to salvage it.

The worst is when you’re hand-bottling a bottle like this: when you cork it, it explodes, showering you with shards of glass and wine.

posts.name: Andrew Hamilton
posts.cooked: That’s an experience I’ve not had with my Durand Alan, thanks for sharing. I will say that my one criticism of the Durand is the fact that the ah-so prongs are quite bulky/thick. The last old bottle I opened I used the Durand corkscrew and a bog standard Monopol Ah-So. It worked a treat.
